entry.title: Re: Standard Value calculation Via CAPP- an Overview
entry.content:
Here is the response to the question raised by the colleague above.

Question:"Hi,
I wonder if that kind of PP via CAPP method is the right for us, as we have some 15.000 different materials going trough production yearly? Does it mean that we have to define 15.000 different calculations?
Can you give me any tip?
Thanks a lot"

Answer: As i know the CAPP method is created at the Work Center/Operation level in the routing. This means that the mathematical calculation/formula is defined at the production line level. If 15,000 different products pass through the same line, you will have only one type of calculation for that line.

entry.title: In SAP CDS Views, the global search functionality is not working for calculated or derived fields.
entry.content:
In SAP CDS Views, the global search functionality is not working for calculated or derived fields.

We have added the annotation @search.searchable:true at the header level and @search.defaultSearchElement at the derived/calculated field level. Additionally, we attempted to separate the calculation field into a view entity and integrated it with another root entity where the search is applied. However, this approach also did not resolve the issue.

The system is throwing a dump with the error TRIGGER_DATABASE_FAILURE_DUMP.

Could you please advise if there is any workaround to make the global search work for calculated or derived fields in CDS views?

entry.title: Re: SAP Enterprise Threat Detection 2.0, SP07!
entry.content: Thank you, Lingscheid for sharing the details of SAP Enterprise Threat Detection product. Would you please advise if customers have implemented the latest version noted above.

entry.title: Costing Sheet Configuration Made Easy
entry.content:
If you're an overhead accountant configuring the costing sheet for the first time or you need a refresher on the topic, our FAQ and updated documentation can help. The FAQ provides a concise guide to some of the most common questions about the Define Costing Sheets configuration activity. Whether you're looking to maintain different overhead rates, add new percentage-based or quantity-based overhead rates, or define credits per company code or plant, this FAQ has got you covered.  

One of the key highlights of this FAQ are quick demos. These demos are designed to give you a step-by-step demo of the process on the UI. They offer a visual walkthrough that can be incredibly helpful. Note that the demos are in English and don't include audio.

Also check out our updated costing sheet documentation: Costing Sheet  

If you think there's a question missing or you need further clarification, don't hesitate to use the Comments feature on the SAP Help Portal.

Feel free to also drop your comments and feedback under this post. We highly appreciate it and want to continuously improve our documentation deliverables for you.

entry.title: Re: FiOri Apps Reference Library API
entry.content: Does anyone know how the releaseID (S27OP, S28OP etc.) are calculated?

entry.title: Re: New Videos on DMS with SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content:
Just to finish the last thought.

You can use Get DMS Folder Content activity to get the contents of the folder you uploaded to. And from the output of that you can create a script (input of type Any, output of type string) with the following code (where "cat.jpeg" is replaced with the name of your file or variable containing it). 

var myobj = inputObject.find(item => item.name === "cat.jpeg");
return myobj.objectId;

entry.title: Re: How To reset SAP HANA Cloud Connector(SCC)'s built-in user(e.g. Administrator) password
entry.content: With Linux, it's just the same. You can use a command like "echo TopSecret | sha256sum | tr [[:lower:]] [[:upper:]]" to generate the password line.

I am leaving you my email address in case you want us to show it to you: jonathan.alaminos@seidor.com

Best regards,
Jonathan Alaminos,

entry.title: Re: Change Documents in IW32
entry.content:
Hello Giuseppi! thanks for the thelp until now

I created the External Operation at 13:03 h, then i got back to the header level and waited until 13:10 h
to save the order to check the behavior of the changes, and that is the result.

Obs: I don´t think its something related to 'Define follow-up actions', because it only happens with one specific plant, out of many.

entry.title: Re: MTA App with UI5 - Running same application isolated...
entry.content:
Your first question is the same that i've done when the requirement was raised 🙂

BTW, i tried to change.... almost everything is dynamic... the last point was this one trying to refresh WZ...

 "messages": {
      "amazing.application.that.i.developed": {
        "error": [
          "Duplicate \"sap.app/id\", id must be unique in subaccount."
        ],
        "warning": []
      },

  so... now, i have a new subaccount 🙂 
The problem is that you need to touch the manifest.json for each app (almost under what i've seen) and you have to do it with external scripts. 

Now is a problem of the past!

entry.title: Re: End of Maintenance for Basic Authentication
entry.content: @PL1 Thank you very much for your reply. Additional Resource has 2 links, one is for the learning and one is for any login. Customers would not know which logins the article is subjected to just by looking at the additional resource. Also the article mentions in the headline that the support ends in June, then the detailed timeline shows end of deployment and end of the maintenance day. The description does not specify what customers expect by those dates. What can customers do to get precise and detailed info?

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA Public Cloud TM and WM Integration for Inbound Purchase Order
entry.content:
Hi Thomas,

let me make you aware of Customer Influence 316053, Inbound transportation planning with warehouse management. If not yet done, you can vote for this customer influence request. This helps us to understand the demand by customers and to prioritize the developments accordingly. When the development of this topic is planned, we will update the status of the customer influence request accordingly (and in addition publish a planned innovation on the roadmap).

With best regards,
Thomas Rösch
SAP SE

entry.title: File system read write monitoring for SAP EMployee Central Payroll system
entry.content:
Hello Community,

We have a unique scenario involving one of our ABAP systems hosted in SAP SF Cloud.

We are looking to monitor the users who read, write, or update files in the EC Payroll directory located at “/sapmnt/<SID>/Customerdata/../<foldername>”. Specifically, we need a feature that can provide a list of users who perform these actions on UNIX file systems. The system under scope is Employee Central Payroll.

This is not possible using Cloud ALM.

If anyone has implemented this type of monitoring in their landscape, your insights and support would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you in advance for your assistance.

entry.content: Was the update created by same user id (else some periodic background job ?)

entry.title: Re: Responsibility Management: Create Your Teams (2/5)
entry.content:
First of all, thank you for the great explanation! 

Is there any way to add an extra field like "Cost Center" under Responsibility Definitions? Unfortunately, in the Manage Workflow Purchase Order App, we can't select "Accounting Object Responsible" like we can in the Manage Workflow Purchase Requisition App.

#flexible workflow #cost center #controlling #purchasing

entry.title: Re: How to enable the create button in the Flexible Progr...
entry.content:
Hi, MattDion.

As a temporary solution it is correct. But, visually it is not pleasant to have all the items in edit mode.

However, the solution of @mariusfreitag is nice. But it also has certain drawbacks. As commented by @anilnagvanshi on the Youtube channel.

"What i observed in this video, is that even though the developer is using a custom button to create a record in the Table in draft state, the moment he edits the draft record or even if he edits the existing records, there is a separate "Create" button that comes up in the Header of the Table. Now two things related to this behaviour:
1. If the Table rendered by Fiori elements' Line Item annotation in a Custom Page, has a "create" button, why doesn't it show up in the first place, why only in the scenario when the particular record of the entity is in Edit mode.
2. If the developer had to use a custom button to create entity in draft state, he should've disabled the upcoming of "Create" button when the record is in editing mode."

On the other hand, when trying to use the cancel button I got the following error:

And it didn't show me the standard message either, so I made an adjustment as shown below:

Preview:

Is there a way to make the create button appear right away? Without having to enter edit mode? Otherwise, is it possible to edit just one item and not all of them?

entry.title: Mentoring - Meant to Be!
entry.content:
Mentoring is a term that's commonly heard but not always fully understood. In a technical team, it's often seen as simply grooming someone on a topic, sharing knowledge, and helping them improve—these aspects are certainly part of it.

However, as you progress in your career, there comes a time when you seek advice or validation for your thoughts and approaches. At this stage, these conversations become more personal and are shared only with a select group of trusted individuals.

This is where the mentoring offered true support while grooming and preparing to take on more.

Some key takeaways from your reflection are:

  1. Collaborative Thinking: Even when someone feels competent, discussing ideas and decisions with a mentor can reveal new insights or considerations. External review can bring out nuances that may have been overlooked, ensuring a more thorough approach.

  2. Specific Feedback and Perspectives: Rather than generic advice, mentoring can provide actionable suggestions and fresh perspectives that are directly relevant to the individual’s current challenges. This can be a catalyst for improvement and helps guide the person in a clear direction.

  3. Motivation and Confidence Building: Mentoring is not just about providing technical guidance—it’s also about being a source of encouragement. Mentors often play the role of confidence boosters, reminding their mentees of their capabilities, even when self-doubt creeps in.

  4. A Trusted Support System: The most valuable aspect of mentoring, as you’ve pointed out, is having someone you can trust to validate your work. These relationships often become personal, where mentees can seek both professional advice and emotional reassurance.

  5. A Constant Source of Feedback: Having someone who can validate your work provides a level of reassurance that is crucial for personal growth. It's comforting to have a mentor who can offer constructive criticism or simply give the green light when things are on the right track.

#SLIThoughtLeadership

entry.title: Navigating Digital Healthcare: Using Zoom for Career Growth in SAP
entry.content:
Understanding the Role of Digital Tools in Healthcare Careers

From electronic health records (EHRs) to telehealth platforms, digital tools have transformed healthcare by enhancing accessibility and efficiency. SAP plays a key role in managing healthcare operations, while Zoom provides seamless communication—bridging the gap between professionals, patients, and IT systems.

With hospitals and clinics adopting SAP-based solutions for scheduling, billing, and data analytics, integrating Zoom can optimize workflows and create a more connected healthcare environment.

The Importance of Telehealth in Modern Healthcare Delivery

Telehealth has revolutionized the way healthcare services are delivered. With the rise of remote consultations and digital health solutions, platforms like Zoom have become essential.

Why Telehealth Matters Today

  • Accessibility: Patients in remote areas can connect with specialists without traveling.
  • Efficiency: Reduces wait times and streamlines consultations.
  • Cost-Effectiveness: Minimizes operational costs while improving patient care.
  • Data-Driven Insights: Integrated tools within SAP can analyze patient data for better decision-making.

Can I use Zoom for telehealth? Absolutely! Many healthcare institutions use Zoom for HIPAA-compliant video consultations, virtual care coordination, and training. 

Leveraging Zoom for Career Advancement

Enhancing Communication Skills through Zoom

Clear and effective communication is essential in healthcare. Whether you’re discussing patient cases, training junior staff, or collaborating on SAP projects, Zoom enhances interaction in the following ways:

  • Virtual Meetings & Webinars: Conduct discussions with experts worldwide.
  • Presentation & Training Skills: Use Zoom’s screen-sharing and whiteboard features.
  • Real-Time Collaboration: Improve teamwork in SAP-driven workflows.

Building Professional Networks in SAP Environments

Networking is key to career growth. Zoom enables professionals to:

  • Join Virtual Conferences & Webinars on SAP in healthcare.
  • Connect with Industry Experts & Recruiters through online events.
  • Engage in Knowledge-Sharing Sessions with SAP professionals.

Integrating Zoom with SAP for Healthcare

SAP provides critical healthcare solutions such as:

  • SAP Patient Management: Optimizing hospital workflows.
  • SAP S/4HANA for Healthcare: Managing financial and operational data.
  • SAP SuccessFactors: Handling HR and training needs.

Benefits of Zoom Integration in SAP Systems

Feature

Description

User-Friendly Interface

Simplifies scheduling and managing virtual meetings.

Data Security

Ensures compliance with HIPAA and other regulations.

Real-Time Collaboration

Facilitates instant communication among healthcare teams.

Telehealth Integration

Enhances virtual consultations for patients and providers.

Training & Webinars

Offers educational sessions for SAP users in healthcare.

Key Features of Zoom and SAP Integration

  1. Secure Video Conferencing – End-to-end encryption ensures compliance.
  2. Seamless Workflow Integration – Zoom integrates with SAP for scheduling and record-keeping.
  3. Automated Meeting Management – SAP users can set up and track meetings within their workflows.

Training and Development Opportunities

Online Courses and Certifications for SAP Professionals

Staying ahead in healthcare IT requires continuous learning. Here are some top courses:

  • SAP Healthcare Solutions Training (offered by SAP Learning Hub)
  • Zoom for Healthcare Workshops (Zoom Academy)
  • Telehealth Certification Programs (American Telemedicine Association)

Workshops on Effective Use of Zoom in Healthcare Settings

Many healthcare institutions now offer hands-on Zoom training, including:

  • How to Conduct Secure Virtual Consultations
  • Advanced Zoom Features for Medical Training
  • Best Practices for Patient Engagement on Zoom

Case Studies: Successful Implementations of Zoom in Healthcare

Telemedicine Success Stories

Example 1: Mayo Clinic – The Mayo Clinic leveraged Zoom’s HIPAA-compliant telehealth solutions to provide remote consultations, reducing patient wait times by 40%.

Example 2: Cleveland Clinic – Integrated Zoom into their SAP-based scheduling system, optimizing physician availability and improving patient satisfaction scores.

Enhancing Patient Engagement through Virtual Meetings

Hospitals and clinics have reported:

  • Higher patient satisfaction with remote check-ups.
  • Increased engagement through interactive health sessions.
  • Improved accessibility for patients with mobility issues.

Future Trends in Digital Healthcare

The Growing Role of AI and Machine Learning in Telehealth

  • AI-Powered Diagnostics – AI-driven tools integrated with SAP and Zoom for early disease detection.
  • Predictive Analytics – SAP HANA using patient data to optimize treatments.
  • Virtual Health Assistants – AI-powered chatbots assisting in telehealth appointments.

Predictions for the Future of Zoom in Healthcare Environments

  • More advanced integrations with SAP for automated workflows.
  • Enhanced AI capabilities for personalized patient care.
  • Increased use of AR/VR for remote diagnostics and surgery assistance.

Conclusion: Embracing Digital Transformation in Healthcare Careers

The intersection of Zoom, SAP, and healthcare is revolutionizing the industry, offering professionals new opportunities for career growth, improved patient outcomes, and streamlined operations. By embracing digital tools, virtual communication, and continuous learning, healthcare professionals can stay ahead in this dynamic landscape.

Are you ready to take your healthcare career to the next level with Zoom and SAP? The future is digital—make sure you’re part of it!

FAQs

  1. Can I use  zoom for telehealth ?
    Yes! Zoom provides HIPAA-compliant solutions for virtual consultations and remote healthcare services.
  2. How does Zoom integrate with SAP in healthcare?
    Zoom integrates with SAP systems for scheduling, patient management, and real-time communication, enhancing healthcare workflows.
  3. What are the benefits of using Zoom for healthcare professionals?
    Zoom enhances communication, facilitates telehealth, improves training, and enables secure virtual meetings within SAP environments.
  4. Are there training programs for using Zoom in healthcare?
    Yes, Zoom offers healthcare-focused training, and SAP provides courses on digital transformation in medical settings.
  5. How does AI impact the future of Zoom in healthcare?
    AI-driven tools will enhance virtual diagnostics, automate meeting management, and integrate predictive analytics into telehealth.
  6. What are some real-world examples of Zoom in healthcare?
    Hospitals like Mayo Clinic and Cleveland Clinic use Zoom for remote consultations, training, and integrated scheduling with SAP.
  7.  How can I advance my career using Zoom and SAP? By mastering digital communication, taking specialized training, networking in virtual SAP events, and leveraging Zoom’s features for collaboration.

entry.title: Empowering New Talent: Two Innovative Projects for a Brighter Future at SAP
entry.content:
Introduction

As a new SAP employee and an immigrant who has faced the obstacles of integrating into a global company, I've spent a lot of time thinking about how we might better help and empower new colleagues. Drawing on my own experiences and a thorough examination of our current processes, I am thrilled to present three project concepts for the #ETB that I feel can make a significant difference. These initiatives: SAP Success Stories Hub, and Innovators' Lab  are my recommendations for improving the onboarding and integration experience at SAP, particularly for people who, like me, come from varied cultural backgrounds.

 

SAP Success Stories Hub: Inspiring Growth Through Shared Journeys

The Challenge:

New employees frequently lack a clear and accessible professional growth roadmap. This disparity is even more obvious for immigrant workers, who may struggle to identify role models from similar cultural or geographical origins. The lack of apparent success stories can leave many people without the motivation or assistance they need to advance their careers at SAP.

My Proposal:

I suggest establishing the SAP Success Stories Hub, a centralized digital portal that curates compelling case studies, interviews, and multimedia tales from SAP workers of all jobs, locations, and cultural backgrounds. By emphasizing varied routes and crucial milestones, this hub will allow new hires, particularly immigrants, to connect with success stories that are similar to their own experiences. Career timelines and discussion forums are interactive features that will provide realistic mentorship recommendations and encourage new employees with a clear, motivating roadmap for personal and professional advancement.

 

Innovators’ Lab: Unleashing Fresh Ideas

The Challenge:

New employees often enter SAP brimming with innovative ideas, yet the absence of a dedicated platform can result in these creative insights being overlooked. Without a structured environment for collaboration, many potential initiatives that align with our digital transformation goals remain unrealized.

My Proposal:

I propose creating an Innovators' Lab, a dedicated virtual and physical space where new employees can submit ideas, collaborate with cross-functional teams, and develop innovative solutions. Regular innovation challenges, hackathons, and brainstorming sessions, led by professional mentors and backed by leadership, will provide the resources and environment required for innovative thinking. This place will enable new personnel to put their fresh insights into actionable projects, thereby driving SAP's continuous improvement and agile innovation.

 

Conclusion

As a new hire at SAP, I’ve taken the time to reflect on how we can make the onboarding journey more enriching, especially for those coming from diverse backgrounds. The Innovators’ Lab and SAP Success Stories Hub are my humble proposals to foster collaboration, knowledge-sharing, and inspiration among employees.

That being said, SAP is a vast and innovative company, and similar initiatives may already exist. If that’s the case, I’d love to learn more about them and explore how I can contribute. I’m open to any feedback, insights, or guidance on how to get involved. So let’s connect and continue the conversation!  😊

entry.title: Re: The “onAfterDataEntryProcess” event in SAP Analytics Cloud Optimized Story
entry.content:
@dedefelrodrigues 

You are basically right - it is important to consider timeouts / leaving before the action was triggered.

Potentially there is a smart way to detect this timeout and in worst case run the DA with full scope, but especially in large volume cases this might bring significant benefit if a DA needs to be run when changing the tab.

entry.title: BW Bridge: DTP Filter doesn't fetch any records but there is data to be processed
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I have a data flow from one standard ADSO to another standard ADSO via an InfoSource. Both transformations have SQLScript Expert Routines. I went ahead and created a full DTP with a filter on 0CALMONTH. When I run the DTP, no data is retrieved nor processed. If I run the full DTP without a filter, the load runs fine. I'm sure the source has data for the selection because I'm able to see records for the same filter selection in the HANA Database Explorer. If I create an intermediate ADSO to "stage" the data inbetween, both transformations routines run fine and are able to load data to and from the intermediate ADSO. Any ideas what might be causing the issue with the InfoSource flow?

entry.title: SWPM crashing on SLES
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I am trying to run a clean installation of SAP ERP 6.0 on SLES 15 SP16. However SWPM is crashing:

I digged up the entire internet:
- Yes, we have 20 GB swap configured
- Yes, we have about 1.8 GB free space in /tmp (everybody says check if you have enough free space, but noone cares to say HOW MUCH is enough!)
- Yes, we have memory, core file, stack and data set to Unlimited for the root user launching SWPM.

Do I need any specific Java version installed ? If yes - WHICH one ?
What else could be causing it ? I have completely run out of ideas...

Many thanks for any useful advice you could give!

entry.title: SAC Error pop ups - Source filters could not be propagated because of a query error.
entry.content:
Hello Everyone, After the recent SAC release on Feb 15 2025, We face below errors on clicking the table cells which uses OnSelect() and Linked Analysis to display details on another linked table using separate Models.

But ondouble click it works fine. 

Note- Before this release this was working fine.

 

Error:

The data cannot be displayed: the query process was stopped before it finished because it returned too many records. Please try filtering your data.
Correlation ID: 24455707-2635-4662-a411-821124669213

 

Source filters could not be propagated because of a query error.
Correlation ID: 19556557-7240-4518-9416-966285101528

entry.title: Re: How Can I buy SAP SQL Anywhere 17 License
entry.content: Interesting. Thanks RiclWash. Therefore the SAP Store change sounds more like the shutting down of the e-commerce provider rather than a SAP decision. Certainly we hope the "request a quote" button provides an ongoing opportunity to buy SQL Anywhere licenses (on comparable terms). This said, I think my "little enthusiasm" comment remains, and if correct, that my encouraged win-win-win suggestion comes to pass.

entry.title: Re: SAC Deployments via APIs
entry.content:
So we found a solution to this problem. The trick is to not pass CSRF token itself in step 4. rather there is an intermediate step to perform a "GETCSRFTOKEN" request explicitly and pass on the "Cookies" received in response header to the next request and keep CSRF token parameter blank in next request.

With that, any authentication always follows above methodology involving a CSRF token request

entry.title: Re: MEASUREMENT POINT MAINTENANCE PLAN DUE BASED ON LAST...
entry.content: So would I add a PRT for that measurement point to the operation on the tasklist for the service PM?

entry.title: Re: BW4HANA Open ODS view - master data
entry.content:
1. Apply Filtering in BW Queries (Analysis for Office, Query Designer)

In Query Designer, create a restricted key figure or filter that explicitly filters records based on a key date. Use a filter condition such as:

Valid_From <= Key Date AND

Valid_To >= Key Date

Example: If your Open ODS View has fields: VALID_FROM (timestamp or date) VALID_TO (timestamp or date) CUSTOMER_ID Then, create a filter on CUSTOMER_ID where: [VALID_FROM] <= [Key Date] AND [VALID_TO] >= [Key Date]

This ensures that only the correct master data records valid at the key date are used.

2. Use a Calculation View in SAP HANA (If Using HANA-Based Open ODS)

If your Open ODS View is based on an SAP HANA table/view, create a calculated column that dynamically checks whether a record is valid on a given key date.

Sample SQL code: CASE WHEN CURRENT_DATE BETWEEN VALID_FROM AND VALID_TO THEN 'Active' ELSE 'Inactive' END AS Valid_Status You can then filter the Open ODS View to include only rows where Valid_Status = 'Active'.

3. Transform the Data in a Composite Provider or ADSO If Open ODS Views alone do not work, you can create a Composite Provider or ADSO to store the master data with pre-applied filtering.

Load the data into an ADSO with a calculated field that applies the same filtering logic (VALID_FROM <= KEY DATE AND VALID_TO >= KEY DATE).

Then, use this ADSO in reports instead of the Open ODS View.

4. Consider Using CALDAY Instead of Timestamps If you’re using timestamps (YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS), BW queries might struggle with date-based filtering. Convert timestamps into CALDAY format (YYYYMMDD) so you can use standard date-based filters in BW. Finally to make Open ODS Views respect the validity period:

Apply explicit filter conditions in BW Queries (VALID_FROM <= KEY DATE AND VALID_TO >= KEY DATE).

Use a HANA Calculation View if working with HANA-based data. If filtering in Open ODS View is insufficient, store the data in a Composite Provider or ADSO.

Convert timestamps to CALDAY for easier date-based filtering.

Hope it should resolve the issue.

entry.title: Re: ML_GET_BLOCKED_INFO
entry.content:
@regdomaratzki Apologies for not answering sooner. We are running into this issue frequently now, twice in the last two days.

Below are the connection parameters:

-c "DSN=ESEAWS_17;uid=fast_ul;pwd=***"
-o C:\iany\ESEAWS-17.log
-zp
-on 10m
-zu+
-x tls(port=26609;tls_type=rsa;identity=C:\iAny\FE\Certificates\FN_2021.id;identity_password=***)
-zs ESEAWS-17
-zw 10
-w 10
-wu 10
-ct 5
-dt
-v

 

Below is the DSN information.  We found the user id in the DSN settings "fast" or "fast_ul" don't appear to make a difference with the service connecting as the one in the service is the one that is actually used.

entry.title: Re: F4 help for Application Job in Public Cloud (2502 release)
entry.content:
Hi @abhishekd 

Thanks for this post. It's exactly what I was looking for.

Just one question, is there a way to translate the text of the parameters in the Job Catalog Entry? In my case, I have a client whose users will the Application Job app in English and French.

Regards.

entry.title: Issue with RAP singleton instance handle validation and message propagation in RAP OData V4
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We are working with a RAP Singleton OData V4 service that includes validations to restrict the SAVE operation using the FAILED structure under FOR VALIDATE ON SAVE.

 

 

APPEND VALUE #( %tky = ls_transactional_data-%tky ) TO  failed-ProjectSBUAlloc.
        APPEND VALUE #( %tky = ls_transactional_data-%tky
              %state_area = 'VALIDATE_PERCENT'
              %path-ProjectSBUAllocAll = CORRESPONDING #( ls_transactional_data )
              %element-distkey = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
              %msg     = new_message(

                  id       =  'ZTEST'
                  number   =  000
                  severity = if_abap_behv_message=>severity-error "CONV #( 'E')
                  v1       = 'Percentage value cannot be greater than 100'
                  )
        ) TO  reported-ProjectSBUAlloc.

 

 

This works as expected in the Eclipse ADT preview, but we are facing an issue when accessing it via the Fiori app.

 

The custom validation message does not appear on the UI; instead, only the framework error (RAP_DSP_REQUEST/023) is returned. Despite properly updating the FAILED AND REPORTED structures, the validation failures are not being correctly tied to the singleton instance, unlike regular entities.

The RAP framework is overriding the message, preventing it from being properly surfaced in the response.

What would be the best approach to ensure that our custom validation messages are correctly displayed in the UI when using RAP?

Any suggestions would be greatly appreciated!

entry.title: From Surviving To Thriving: How To Bring Out The Best In Your Business
entry.content:
For years, business leaders have been chasing the same vision of an ideal operation: seamless order fulfillment, smoothly running supply chains, effortless commerce, and consistently balanced finances. However, with disruptions leaving businesses scrambling and critical data trapped in outdated and siloed systems, achieving that vision has become increasingly challenging.

Here’s the problem: most organizations aren’t built for this vision. But it’s not workforce skills or leadership ambitions that are holding them back—it’s an infrastructure that can’t keep up with today’s demands.

The days of siloed transactional systems—where technology simply recorded sales, tracked expenses, and processed orders—are fading fast. Instead, businesses need systems that can react to change instantly, use AI to automate decisions, connect data across silos for a single source of truth, and provide real-time visibility across the entire value chain.

 

Know the signs of falling into the complexity trap

Many businesses are stuck in a complexity trap without realizing it.

At first, everything is working perfectly well. Systems are in place, teams are meeting goals, and operations are moving forward.

But over time, businesses and the systems that support them can get mired in technical debt. A mix of legacy technology, customized add-ons, and modern solutions are patched together over years, often without a clear integration strategy. Operational cracks begin to show as processes slow down, decisions are misinformed, and bottlenecks emerge.

What once felt like a well-oiled machine now feels like an uphill battle. Teams waste hours searching for information spread across multiple systems. Different departments using their own “niche” systems suffer from inefficiencies and miscommunication. Business leaders lack the real-time insights and 360-degree visibility necessary to make effective, well-informed strategies. And by the time a decision is made, the opportunity has likely already passed, or the problem has escalated.

 

Break free with a new era of enterprise management

Organizations thriving in today’s landscape are taking a step beyond patching up old systems. They're redefining enterprise management by integrating automation, AI-driven insights, and continuous connectivity.

Unlocking new levels of agility and efficiency, this transformation is based on a three-layer intelligent enterprise model spanning transactional systems, transformational insights, and autonomous AI agents.

Transactional systems that break down silos

Businesses still need stable, reliable systems to handle everyday processes, including finance, procurement, and supply chain management. These Cloud ERP based systems work together to form a strong foundation for running business fundamentals smoothly.

Transformational insights to make informed decisions

Beyond collecting data, thriving organizations are activating its full potential with AI. The combination of AI and analytics brings real-time visibility and clean, accurate insights that empower leaders to make the right decisions at the right time, instead of relying on outdated reports.

Autonomous AI agents to augment and empower humans

By leveraging machine learning and automation, businesses can eliminate tedious manual processes. These AI-driven systems act like intelligent assistants, continuously optimizing workflows, predicting disruptions, and executing tasks faster and more accurately than human intervention alone.

With this foundational change, businesses operate with a completely different rhythm where:

  • Data isn’t just collected—it’s continuously analyzed and acted upon. By making decision-making faster and more agile businesses can pivot instantly when supply chain disruptions hit.
  • Teams don’t just react—they anticipate and adapt in real time. Such an environment allows collaboration across finance, sales, and operations with a single, unified source of truth.
  • Leaders don’t just monitor performance—theyoptimize it as conditions change. This includes using AI to automate routine tasks and free up employees to focus more on innovation and strategic growth.
  • Growth isn’t just incremental—it’sscalable and sustainable. This capability is supported when systems are flexible enough to expand and evolve as business needs shift.
  • Sustainability isn’t an afterthought—it’s embedded into operations. AI-driven insights and process optimization empower businesses to reduce waste, lower emissions, and build ethical, transparent supply chains.

Step into the future with intelligence, resilience, and confidence

Breaking free from the complexity trap requires a shift from reactive to proactive, from fragmented to integrated, and from struggling to keep up to leading the way. And it's achievable for every business, regardless of size.

Any business that embraces change can integrate AI, automation, and intelligent decision-making into daily operations to move beyond survival mode and into a future where agility, efficiency, and innovation drive success.

The technology is here, and the opportunity is now. Will your business lead the change or struggle to keep up?

Discover how SAP Business Suite can help you bring out the best of your business.

entry.title: Content Federation from S/4 HANA to SAP BTP Build Workzone opens on premise FLP in new tab
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

we have setup BTP build workzone with content federation from on-premise S/4HANA system (with Exposure Version V2). When the user click on a (exposed) tile in the workzone, a new tab is opened and the app is shown in the on-premise FLP. PWhen the user clicks on back or home button the users stays in the FLP on-premise and is not navigating back to the workzone. Now users can open the apps that are on the on-premise FLP, but the users should start the apps from the workzone. In note 3345119 

If this is how it should work what is the benefit of exposing content to the workzone? In that case a link to the on-premise FLP would then be sufficient or do we miss some configuration?

Thanks in advance

Lee

entry.title: SAP BDC integration with Datasphere and SAC
entry.content:
My client wants to understand how SAP BDC integrates with SAP Datasphere (SAP DS) and SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC). Can someone provide some uses cases please? We have both SAP DS and SAC in our landscape.

PS: Client has been using SAP BW 7.5 and HANA for long time. Slowly building SAP BW and HANA objects in SAP DS. SAP S/4HANA private cloud implementation to kickoff soon. 

So given the above details i want to understand how SAP BDC will be of any use to my client.

entry.title: Execute work item Link to Fiori My Inbox using RSWUWFML2 Report
entry.content:
How to send a My Inbox link using the RSWUWFML2 report?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Execute work item Link to Fiori My Inbox using RSWUWF...
entry.content:
Hello,

My Inbox is not supported with RSWUWFML2, so it is not possible to provide a link to access the 'My Inbox' application in standard. This report can only serve SBWP.

With Extended Notifications, you can link to the 'My Inbox' application, but not directly to the work item in standard. See KBA 2892592 - WF Notifications: Generate link to My Inbox.

Please check the following KBA for detailed information on Extended Notifications configuration:
1646056 - Quick Start Guide to Extended Notifications

 

Best regards.

entry.title: Re: Thresholds Prevent Unintended Deletion of Users when Provisioning with SAP Cloud Identity Servic
entry.content: Is there any chance to see in the logs, which entities are affected by the threshold and therefore not being deleted?

entry.title: Re: End of Maintenance for Basic Authentication
entry.content: I’d refer to these entries: Migration to SAP Cloud Identity Authentication (IAS/IPS) - Innovation Alert & Open Office Hours | Learning Native Login Deletion - 2H 2024 - Innovation Alert.

entry.title: Re: Change Documents in IW32
entry.content:
The update was created automatically when saving the order, is the field BEDNR (EBAN) always for what i see.

 

This field is sent to EBAN when the Purchase requisition is created saving the order

entry.title: Re: How Can I buy SAP SQL Anywhere 17 License
entry.content: Well, currently SQL Anywhere is also part of other SAP products (IQ, Data Lake, SAP HANA Remote Data Sync?, not that I'm familiar with those), so a complete "buy-out" seems not realistic IMHO. Of course I strongly share the "SAP as a company shows little enthusiasm for SQL Anywhere" impression vs. the personal "overwhelming enthusiams". I currently work primarily with MS SQL Server, and man, do I miss many all too familiar SQL Anywhere features day by day...

entry.title: Re: exchange rate function module
entry.content:
You can use;

CONVERT_TO_LOCAL_CURRENCY

            " date  = e_ceban-badat
            " foreign_amount   = e_ceban-gswrt
            " foreign_currency = e_ceban-waers
            " local_currency   = 'TRY'

entry.title: Re: Condition Contract - Automatic pricing conditions det...
entry.content:
Hi @Miche1984 

You cannot predefine the condition records for the Accruals/Settlement condition types for a condition contract. You have to maintain the condition values in the condition contract only.

Regards

Hari

entry.title: Screen Personas change background color at java script event onFocus
entry.content:
Dear Screen Personas fans,

we are preparing ZEBRA MC9400 for the use of screen personas. We could adjust everything except the change of the background color when the focus is set on an input field.

Unfortunately even in flavors this event is not supported.

Any idea how to do that with a script?

THX, Peter

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA Public Cloud TM and WM Integration for Inbound Purchase Order
entry.content:
@thomas_roeschthank you for the response. I did see that topic previously and submitted a note, so hopefully will see an update soon.

Are you aware of any alternate solutions that have been provided to customers? We've been working on a potential solution by adjusting our business process, but I was curious what other customers may have implemented.

entry.title: Problematic "Unnamed Object" in Fiori Elements app powered by RAP endpoint
entry.content:
Hello dear colleagues,

I have a scenario where I expose data from a table using RAP. Some fields that are part of the table key can be empty. For such fields, they look good on the OData V4 provided, they come empty as expected.

The problem is: we created a Fiori Elements app on top of this OData service, and when we look at the resulting table, all fields that should be displayed empty have the text "Unnamed Object" on them. This happens in multiple fields in multiple rows.

The columns in red are all part of the table key (and they are allowed to be empty), while the column in green is not part of the table key. So only the columns used in the table key display the unwanted "Unnamed Object" text when empty.

If you have any ideas or insights, it will be more than appreciated.

Thanks a lot!
Kind regards.

entry.title: Re: How to Add Custom Fields to Supplier Invoice Template Using Coding Block Business Context?
entry.content:
Thank you for your input, Sharon.
Regarding the new field, how or where could a custom logic be applied to set validation rules? For example: UCOI Numerical where the last digit should be zero, or other validations, that would prevent saving the invoice to be registered.

entry.title: Re: Joule speaks ABAP!
entry.content:
SAP Partners; register to our upcoming webinar SAP BTP Partner Community Live – Ask Me Anything on ABAP Development with Joule, our generative AI copilot:

https://partneredge.sap.com/en/library/education/psd/2025/jan/e_oe_te_w_PSD_WEB_00008933.html

Regards,

Jose

entry.title: In the inbox, why " 'user' tagged the product" although it's just a reply?
entry.content:
I'm just wondering why I receive this post in my inbox marked " 'user' tagged the product" although it doesn't seem to be a tagging action, it's just a reply (link to https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-discussions/what-is-the-use-of-display-maintenance-not-allowed/m-p/14020288#M2038550) :

although it's tagged ABAP Development everywhere.

Also, another post was marked more logically "replied to topic" in my inbox (link to https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-discussions/excel-upload-using-rap-application/m-p/14017216#M2038534) :

Any idea why? Could the mark be improved? (to be "replied to topic")

Thanks.

Sandra

entry.title: Workflow development using SAP Cloud Application Programming Model and SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content:
Overview

SAP Build portfolio provides rich set of capabilities to build cloud native applications in SAP Business Technology Platform. Customers and Partners could develop extension scenarios as part of their clean core strategy using SAP Build. It enables fusion development using low-code/pro-code tools and services, developers or line of business experts with different skills set could collaboratively work to build cloud native solutions.

 A full stack application consists of UIs, Workflows, Business logic and Persistency. Of course integration and consumption of APIs, publishing events ,consuming events from multiple applications are common requirements while building a full stack application. SAP Build Process Automation is a low-code solution to model and execute workflows in Business Technology Platform. The low-code editor enable process experts or developers to model workflows based on various use cases. Consuming data models, APIs and events from various applications like S/4 HANA , leads to complex data models and flow logic in workflow models .This leads to many preprocessing steps, complex mapping logics and writing java scripts within the workflow, which are essentially not part of the core process own by the process owners. This makes the workflow models less intuitive for a process owner, future modifications and maintenance of workflow models become more time consuming and error prone.

This blog explains how to build workflow solutions by consuming simplified APIs, Events with business semantics and lightweight data models so that a process expert or developer could build workflows by focusing on the core process logic and keep the rest outside the workflow model. SAP Build portfolio offers services and frameworks to solve the above problem and build robust workflow solutions together with SAP Build Process Automation.  SAP Cloud Application programming Model (CAP),part of SAP Build portfolio providing rich set of capabilities, best practises to define domain models and services, consume and provision APIs out of the box, publish and consume business Events. 

SAP Cloud Application Programing Model (CAP)

CAP is a framework of tools, languages and libraries, enabling developers to focus on domain, service modeling in a language agnostic way by using Core Data Services(CDS) .It provides best practises, integration to other services  enabling developers to focus more on application logic without implementing low level recurring tasks. CAP abstracts many low level technical aspects like authentication, multitenancy, transaction management, service binding, messaging , networking ,audit logging etc enable developers to focus on their domain models and core application logic. This reduce time, effort and naturally reduce the total cost of development. 

CAP -  Build Cloud Native Applications

CAP enable developers to build modularised cloud native solutions and micro services without writing boilerplate code. Some of the key aspects of CAP are

  • Focus on domain and service modeling, providing out of box generic implementations such as CRUD operations. 
  • CDS as the backbone, provides language agnostic way of designing domain models ,services, queries and expressions.
  • Out of the box integration to various BTP services, reduces code and effort.
  • Standard features like Authorization, Messaging, Input validation, Localisation, Audit logging, Multi Tenancy, Extensibility etc are provided out of the box.
  • Ouf of the box integration with Fiori Elements using annotations and supports other UI technologies like SAP UI5, React etc.
  • Supports various Protocols to consume APIs from different applications and expose APIs as OData/REST.
  • Publishing events and consuming events from other  services / business applications.
  • Choice of development environments like Business Application Studio, Visual Studio Code and IntelliJ.
  • Apart from SAP HANA Cloud, supports PostgreSQL to persists data models.
  • Standard plugins for integration with other BTP services like Document Management, Alert Notification Service , Audit Logging etc and plugins contributed by community.
  • Supports java / node.js to implement custom logic such as actions / functions / event handlers or input validations etc.
  • Consuming packages/libraries from NPM / Maven using declarative dependency .
  • Deployment to Cloud Foundry and Kyma.
  • CI/CD pipeline to automate build, test and deployment.

SAP Build Process Automation

 Build Process Automation enable modeling workflows,tasks and automations using low-code development environment. It provides an integrated development environment for developers and process experts to model workflows, decisions and consume APIs from various line of business applications. It is the successor product of SAP Workflow Management and provides better integrations to Decision/Business Rules, Robotic Process Automation. It is possible to consume REST/OData APIs from different line of business applications  as Actions by configuring destinations to the respective end points.

How to leverage CAP while building Workflow solutions in BTP

The following  sections explains the advantages of using CAP while building Workflow Solution and benefit from the out of the box capabilities CAP provides as part of building cloud native solutions. The below solution diagram shows how CAP can be used together with Process Automation while building a workflow solution in SAP BTP. The workflow models and user interfaces are accessing the CAP services as OData / REST APIs and events. The CAP layer provides a semantic layer of APIs and Events to workflows and UIs. This leverage the out of the box capabilities and best practises of CAP and simplify the workflow development.

Domain Models and Services

Every application uses  domain models based on various data types and requires services to provide access to these models based on various constraints. In a workflow solution, the workflow context is based on a json schema and all the activities read or update the context. The data models which are accessed from remote applications or updates from human tasks or APIs are all part of the workflow context. Depending on the requirements, the domain models requires local persistence or they are accessed from remote applications. Either way, the workflows need this in the context while executing workflow steps and post completion for analytical and auditing reasons.

Keeping the workflow context simple and lightweight  has obvious advantages. For eg: keeping the basic data of a purchase order or sales order as key attributes in the process context and access the data models later when it is required. It helps the workflow developer to focus on process logic without consuming complex data models or not performing complex mappings or without writing scripts to manipulate the context. User interfaces used in approval tasks can load the data models based on fine granular authorizations out side the workflow context by using the key attributes available in the workflow context. This will also help those scenarios requires fine granular authorization to access business data .Input validations are performed out of the box based on annotations defined in the data models in CAP. It will also overcome any limitations in the max size of the workflow context.

Create data models in CAP, choose the right persistency solution between HANA and PostgreSQL. Keep the process context light weight by populating only the key attributes of the data model to the process context and access CAP models using CAP services as and when it is required. Approval UIs can access the CAP data models using services. The data models are accessible post completion of the workflow and it can be accessed from other analytical and support applications based on fine granular authorizations.

Security

Authorization concepts in CAP CDS  helps workflow applications to define very fine granular authorization on domain models and services. The workflow context is accessible using a global role or an instance role but global role override the instance role and make it accessible to all. Applications requiring fine granular authorizations based on user groups or more fine granular approach based on specific attributes are possible using CDS authorization.

CDS enable developers to provide access control at entity and service level. There are different annotations for eg:

@readonly/@InsertOnly - at an entity level and applicable for all authenticated users.

@Requires - access control based on user or user groups defined at entity or service level.

@Restrict - access control based on users or user group on all operations based on specific conditions. This can be defined using a generic way like  { grant:<events>, to:<roles>, where:<filter-condition> }.

The 'grant' accepts all CRUD operations, Actions and Functions. The 'to' property enable specific roles to perform the operation. The 'where' enable additional filters based on one or more attributes.

Publishing and Consuming Events

Business applications are emitting events based on various transactions. As an example, SAP S/4 HANA provision business events, which applications can subscribe and consume. In a side-by-side extensibility scenario, these events can be consumed and extend processes in BTP. But often there are some preprocessing steps required before triggering a workflow or consuming them in a workflow. As an example, an approval required only if the Sales Order is of certain type and above certain value. Or changes in a Business Partner should only require one active workflow instance irrespective of number of changes to the same Business Partner object etc. In all these cases the provider system publishing events and the consumer needs to evaluate the conditions before triggering an approval workflow. Implementing all these logic in a workflow model add additional complexity and bring more technical implementation than the actual process flow logic.

Subscribing business events from applications like S/4 HANA using CAP and do the necessary preprocessing steps, add additional business semantics and expose them as a business event to Process Automation  or use the Process Automation REST API to start the workflow. The workflow developer able to consume an Event  with required context and able to build the flow logic without performing additional preprocessing steps.

Provisioning events based on the data models also enable other applications to subscribe those events. CAP enable provisioning of events based on transactional changes or custom events. This will enable applications to subscribe events not directly from the workflow instance but from the underlying CAP data models. Using events based on CRUD operations on the data models, workflows can be started from CAP using custom logic using node.js or java. 

Enriching Process Visibility with CAP Events

Process Visibility is a capability in Process Automation to provide real time visibility into workflows. Process Automation provision workflow events and consumed from process visibility. Process visibility events can be further enriched using events based on CAP domain models using the out of the box integration CAP provides with Event Mesh. for eg: when a PO created or Modified, CAP can trigger an event with required context information  including the PO number and Process Visibility could consume the event.

Consuming Services from other Applications

All business applications expose APIs to access their data models by other applications. They use different protocols like REST ,OData, BAPI, SOAP etc and different representations like json or xml. Often the responses while reading an object or the request body while posting an object contains  complex nested structures. This require additional validations, transformations of data types , initializing or adding default attributes and exception management  after executing the HTTP operations. A workflow logic implementation only requires a subset of the data model but need to implement additional logic as scripts to validate, transform the data etc before posting them the transactional system.

Consuming APIs from business applications in CAP and implement the required validation,  transformation or business logic and expose a simplified interface to Process Automation enable workflow developers to focus more into the flow logic than do the data transformation or validation logic within the workflow model.

User Interfaces

Workflow solutions require user interfaces to start , approve and for other operational purposes. These user interface consume REST APIs provided by Process Automation. But these user interfaces also require access to the underlying data models with fine granular authorizations to make sure the users have the required authorization to view or modify the data. 

CAP provides out of the box support to build SAP Fiori Elements by using annotations in the services. This include out of the box support for Draft ,enable business users to work in multiple sessions without loosing the data they entered in the UIs.

Process Automation approval tasks only support SAP UI5. So it is only possible to use Fiori Elements for workflow start UIs or any other UIs for operational purposes by consuming CAP data models.

Integration with other BTP Services

Document Management

SAP Document Management service provides SDM plugin for java and node.js. Developers can extend their domain models with attachments as child entity using composition. For eg: A purchasing document or sales document entity can have multiple attachments. The documents are accessible in Fiori elements without additional coding and authorized users can perform CRUD operations.

Managing documents together with the domain model, keep the workflow model clean but have relationship to the documents through the domain model attached to the workflow context. This keep the documents part of the domain model and accessible from approval UIs or other applications without having additional implementations in the workflow logic.

Alert Notification Service

Alert Notification Plugin provides notification capabilities through different channels. Alert Notification Plugin in CAP provides out of the box integration and enable business users to consume notifications through their Work Zone portal. For eg: After creating a purchase requisition or sale order, a notification can be created with  purchase requisition ID or sales order ID as part of post processing.  

Audit Logging

The Audit Logging plugin provides out of the box support for logging personal data related operations by annotating the data model and attributes. These annotations enable automatically logging personal data related operations.

Integration to Audit Logging, relieve the Workflow developer from managing personal data and data privacy related aspects in a workflow model, but able to focus on workflow modelling by accessing the respective data models through CAP.

Other key CAP capabilities 

Localization(i18N), Multitenancy, Extensibility etc are some of the key capabilities customers / partners could leverage while building cloud native solutions. These capabilities supported out of the box in CAP and enable application developers to focus on application logic. 

Summary

Cloud Application Programming model provides a rich set of capabilities to simplify application development without writing low level code and  out of the box integration to many platform services. CAP can be used as a semantic layer to access the data models and events with business context from various applications. This provides a simplified access to the domain models , events  and enable the  Workflow developer or Process experts to build the core process flow logic.

entry.title: Re: How to show value help with values for Application Jo...
entry.content:
For anyone looking for Value Helps in Application Jobs, SAP has just added this functionality in Release 2502. See next:

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/f4-help-for-application-job-in-public-cloud-2502-release/bc-p/14020461#M179168

entry.title: Re: SAP SuccessFactors Incentive Management : Organizational Data
entry.content: HI @NagSv Please keep me honest here but I don't believe Incentive Management is offered as a module within SAP SuccessFactors, it is a SAP suite product, hence blog tag should be different if possible to not create confusion for customers. thanks for blogging !

entry.title: Analysis for Office workbook is frozen / loading does not stop
entry.content:
Hi,

we face the issue that an analysis for office workbook gets frozen after data refreshing. It says "data is loaded from server", but loading does not stop.

What makes things weird is: Queries are alright. We can build a new workbook with exactly the same queries, filters, configurations etc. and this new workbook loads data in a few seconds. No problems.

But rebuilding workbooks is time consuming.... We did this a few weeks ago, spending many hours in rebuilding, and now face the same issue.

Has anybody an idea how the original workbook can be fixed? Is there something like a cache which has to be cleared or a workbook identifier which has to be deleted somewhere?

It seems as if the workbook keeps on running somewhere and needs to be interrupted. But we have no idea how to do this.

Grateful for any hints.

Thanks, 

Chris

entry.title: Re: Screen Personas change background color at java scrip...
entry.content: Anyone know how to highlight a field with color wh... - SAP Community

entry.title: SAP Build Apps Application Backend not working at all.
entry.content:
Hello,

I can't get any simple "hello wolrd" app to work.
When i create an simple SAP Build Application Backend, i get errors in the UI.
I get the same "Error Unable to fulfill proxy request" Error in the SAP Build Application.
And I can't create new records to a Entitiy Database.

Is the software curretly working at all ? I'm trying for days to get any App to work at all.
Deployment process also takes hours

 

Failed to load data.
unexpectedError Unable to fulfill proxy request

 

Error Unable to fulfill proxy requestCan't create new records to the Database.Same error in SAP Build Application

entry.title: Re: Live profile view Variable Pay-Enhancement
entry.content: @rkumar1947 Can you share screenshots of the field permissioning screen ? I have built Variable Pay Individual view where I only displayed custom fields in the past.

entry.title: Re: Connect VS Code with SAP Public Cloud - Custom Data Service - Error
entry.content:
Hello,

Is there an update regarding this? It seems it's still not possible to connect VS to a S/4HANA public cloud system.

 

Regards,

entry.title: Location Brazil: DC-e (Electronic Content Declaration)
entry.content: Hey guys, Could anyone tell me if SAP has already released any SAP Notes regarding DC-e (Electronic Content Declaration)?

entry.title: Re: Variable Pay Budget Calculation
entry.content:
Hi @Olga_Skarla 

The idea of Budgets in Variable Pay is that they are based on the individual section target amount since Business component is something that is paid (or not paid if gates are designed and achievements aren't met) but not something a comp planner should be able to change.

A comp planner can pump up the individual payout (but not the business payout) based on guidelines (similar to Compensation guidelines).

What exactly are you trying to achieve with custom field based budgets ?

 

All the best,

Xavier

entry.title: Re: Eligibility Rules not applied to Compensation Profile
entry.content:
Hi @rebeccanussba 

Compensation profile fields should always be in sync with entry level columns as per product design. Can you please share screenshot or recordings of the issue ? We can take a look to see if we spot anything strange and if not then I'll probably advise to create a ticket to support.

All the best,

Xavier

entry.title: SAP HANA Tools Download from SAP Software Download Center
entry.content:
Hi there,

I have a question about the SAP HANA Tools.

Is it possible to download the Tools from the SAP Software Download Center?

For the ADT and BW-Modeling Tools I found this help page: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_BW4HANA/841c96bc79644fcfb9fa7103aad9c15b/d287ce4ec82f4944a9a09380d489f91c.html

But I couldn't find anything for the SAP HANA Tools (Administration, Application Development, Database Development).

We want to switch/update from HANA Studio to Eclipse with add-Ons and our IT security is very strict with download sites. So getting the tools in Eclipse from https://tools.hana.ondemand.com/ is a bit complicated.

I hope to get the tools with an installation package like ADT and BW-MT.

Thanks for your help

entry.title: Re: Bonus Assignment Statement-Adding Person ID and Prefe...
entry.content: Not sure I understand your comment @NicolòC We're talking about standard tags in Bonus Assignment Statements here (similar to the ones that can be used on standard comp statements). Yes there is a way to retrieve any custom data from EC into Comp or Variable Pay and then on to the statements.

entry.title: Feature Request: Flow Function for Opening Apple Subscription Screen
entry.content:
Hi SAP Build App - Community Team,

My name is Denis, and I have been building my application on SAP Build App for the past four years. I’ve witnessed incredible improvements since the transition from AppGyver to SAP, and I couldn’t be happier with the platform.

Many friends and colleagues doubted my decision to base my entire development on a low-code platform, warning me that I might regret it. However, SAP Build App and I proved them wrong—I’ve built my solution entirely on this platform.

Currently, there is only one roadblock preventing me from launching my project: Apple requires me to use their subscription system and open the Apple Subscription Management screen, using stripe for Android/Web.

I have spent the past four weeks searching for a solution—combing through forums, documentation, and Stack Overflow. I even hired iOS developers to help me, but no solution emerged.

My question is: Is there any chance that SAP Build App could introduce a flow function to open the Apple Subscription Management screen?
Reference: Apple Developer Documentation

I believe this feature would be a valuable addition to SAP Build App, helping to dispel the misconception that low-code platforms are only suitable for prototypes. It would empower creators like me to launch production-ready applications that meet platform-specific requirements.

Thank you so much for your time and consideration.

Best regards,
Denis

@Dan_Wroblewski 

@ajmaradiaga 

@beapasch

entry.title: Re: Advanced intercompany Sales - IC Supplier Invoice not...
entry.content: @Crox11 I checked it and it's exist in reference number but the error still exist

entry.title: Re: Unleash Your Inner ABAP Rockstar with Joule for developers , ABAP AI capabilities
entry.content: How do I add the service to my ADT?

entry.title: Re: Feature Request: Flow Function for Opening Apple Subs...
entry.content: @SAPSupport

entry.title: Re: What’s New in SAP HANA Cloud – December 2024
entry.content:
@Sven_Knöpfler - At this time HC only supports inbound connections using private IP endpoints. SAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA db supports inbound connections on GCP using GCP Private Service Connect and on AWS using AWS Private Link. SAP HANA Cloud, Data Lake supports inbound connections on AWS with support for GCP Private Service Connect planned for 2025-QRC2. 

Support for outbound connections from HC instances to remote sources such as Google Big Query is a backlog item that we do not yet have a delivery timeline for.

entry.title: ADRC number for a plant is same in both the clients
entry.content:
We have situation where a plant is created in 100 client and which generated a ADRC number 4000 for in the client, and when moved TR is moved to 200 and if the same 4000 is already assinged to an existing plant in 200 client for same Dev , system is picking the 200 client address for 100 client plant since the ADRC 4000 is assigned the same, 

example- 

Plant ABCD with ADRC number 4000 country code as HU( Hungary ) , when this TR is moved to 200 and ADRC table if ADRNR = 4000 with Country code CA (Canada) then HU plant now have CA as it country code, this doesn't happen for all but only with same ADRNR number

entry.title: Re: API to read and set Story Filters
entry.content:
API to read story filter values

var selectedFilterValues = Application.getFileDataSource("MODELID").getDimensionFilters("DIMENSIONID");

entry.title: Re: SF Compensation - Executive Review: Filter Option For...
entry.content:
Hi @Ajnos 

After review with our product team we are in the process of updating this KBA to remove the sentence stating that this is not recommended for customers. If you think the customer use case can be handled using form view, then there is no reason why you shouldn't use it.

We are also updating the steps to activate it under Resolution since it can be done directly at the template level under Plan Setup> Advanced Settings.

The change should be active within the next few days.

Thanks

Xavier

entry.title: Re: Integration Suite: Automating Email Alerts for Failed Interfaces in SAP CPI
entry.content: @Daggolu_PremSai  very good aproach. I like It.

entry.title: SuccessFactors New People profile - Questions
entry.content:
We are setting up the new people profile and have not been able to find any information on the following:

1. Defaulting employees to the Company Profile and not the Full Profile when they search for someone.  

2. On the Full Profile , employees with no access to see other employee's banking information in RBP see the Banking Card on Personal Information even they do not have access to other employees banking information.   Can this banking card only be given to certain groups like HR?

entry.title: Re: Unleash Your Inner ABAP Rockstar with Joule for developers , ABAP AI capabilities
entry.content:
@Marian_Zeis next delivery for PCE (2025) will also include Joule for developers. Dowporting will follow later this year probably. S4 OnPrem is still under discussion under which circumstances might benefit. ECC is not planned to benefit of AI. The ECC customers may have access to Joule if they use Steampunk. But the issue is that Joule is design to write ABAP Cloud compliant code and the old ECC code is generally not fit for this purpose. 

@Siarljp and @FelixOrtiz you can just try it. It's free and available. If you are a Steampunk or S4/HANA Public Cloud customer you can ask and get it at no extra cost as of today. License: In order to leverage Joule’s capabilities, you need to purchase an additional license: 8019124 – Joule for developers limited promotion, ABAP. Please check SAP Note 3571857 for details. This license is a limited promotion free of charge until September 15th 2025 and can be purchased until August 15th 2025. You may condier reading also this blog writen by @AlexanderRother : https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/joule-speaks-abap/ba-p/14018226

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Apps Application Backend not working at all.
entry.content:
@FreddyG 

I can suspect you might have connection issue with your DB or API which is not reteriving the data.

 

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/developing-an-app-with-sap-build-apps-using-github-api-less-than-5mins/ba-p/14017891

entry.title: Re: Im not able to show potencial rating in compensation...
entry.content:
Hi @ernestogabrielm 

as far as I know only results can be brought to Compensation worksheet columns, not description.

All the best,

Xavier

entry.title: Re: 🚨 SAP Build Apps – Access Denied (403 Forbidden) – S...
entry.content:
@mazenelba@mazenelba @depratihari @Sateesh7 @halley_alphonse 

Please check now, issue is resolved

entry.title: Does Basic Syntax support the Format function?
entry.content:
I am using Basic Syntax.

The following simple formula:
formula = "My Number: " & Round(1234.56,0)
yields:
My Number: 1,235.00

The Round function is rounding to zero decimals but also auto-formatting the result to add a comma and display two decimal places.

I tried:
formula = "My Number: " & Format(Round(1234.56,0), "#,##0")
but it doesn't look like Basic Syntax supports the Format function.

entry.title: Re: Identity and Access Management with Microsoft Entra, Part I: Managing access to SAP BTP
entry.content:
@H_Ettelbrueck Following this blog we set up  authentication as follows: 

Entra ID - (OIDC)- IAS - (OIDC) - App.    This set up works .  

But for Admin Console are you suggesting  to configure Entra ID - (SAML) - IAS admin console system application in IAS?  Is it possible to have both OIDC and SAML against the same EntraID Enterprise App registration?

entry.title: Re: How Can I buy SAP SQL Anywhere 17 License
entry.content: Indeed I've heard this myself, Volker. A nightmare scenario is for SAP to maintain SQL Anywhere as an in-house tool, and not worry about supporting outside users or welcoming new ones. If SAP no longer wished to be burdened with SQL Anywhere as a commercially available and supported product, presumably they could keep and maintain an in-house copy, while forking the commercial version to Appeon.

entry.title: Re: Two-Step Picking in SAP EWM
entry.content:
Hi experts,

Who know if 2-step-picking can we used with basic versión EWM?

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Variable Pay Budget Calculation
entry.content:
Hello @xavierlegarrec , thanks for your answer. We want to allocate the budget for each compensation manager in the system, as we do in the salary plan where we the custom way for the budget. More specific we add with a lookup table the budget amount and we have the total amount which is allocated as it should in the process. In the variable pay form we do the same but we face an issue: we see the budget amount but at the total we see an unexpected amount which we do not know where it comes from. We use results budget. 

Also the final amount of the bonus is 0 into the final pay out field, and we enter an amount into the override field the final pay out takes the same amount with the business calculation amount field. We use only individual calculations and we do not use business goals.

Your guideline would be helpful.

entry.title: How SAP B1 specifically in picking module create information from "bin location allocation - Issue"?
entry.content:
Hi,
I have a little question about how SAP B1 create the information from "bin location allocation - Issue" to show. I would like to recreate this using a queary, I very confuse about which table are consulting.

Thanks.

entry.title: Assignment profile not picking up some users
entry.content:
AP is not picking up some set of users who are eligible for Assignment profile rules (Country and is line manager IS yes). The affected users are same domain and group as per the AP criteria and have been lone managers for long time and have 'yes' in line managers on EC and LMS profile .

 

What could be the reason for this issue ? Please help

entry.title: Re: SAP Standard Kafka Integration Options
entry.content:
If you haven't looked at Datasphere yet, then it would be worthwhile to evaluate the Datasphere support for Kafka: 

Apache Kafka Connections

entry.title: Re: Problematic "Unnamed Object" in Fiori Elements app powered by RAP endpoint
entry.content: By desgin a key is not allowed to be empty, so you may need to set the keys to other fields or create a UUID for the entries as single key field
